---
title: "Bo_Bichette_BIP_2023"
author: "Rayyan Haider"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r libraries, include=FALSE}
# Load libraries
library(baseballr)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(httr)
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
# Bo Bichette MLBAM ID
bo_id <- 666182

# Direct Baseball Savant CSV URL for Bo Bichette 2023 batting Statcast data
url <- paste0(
  "https://baseballsavant.mlb.com/statcast_search/csv?",
  "all=true&hfPT=&hfAB=&hfBBT=&hfPR=&hfZ=&stadium=&hfBBL=&hfNewZones=&",
  "hfGT=R%7CPO%7CS%7C&hfC=&hfSea=2023%7C&hfSit=&hfOuts=&opponent=&",
  "pitcher_throws=&batter_stands=&hfSA=&player_type=batter&hfInfield=&",
  "team=&position=&hfOutfield=&hfRO=&home_road=&",
  "batters_lookup%5B%5D=", bo_id,
  "&game_date_gt=2023-03-30&game_date_lt=2023-10-01&hfFlag=&hfPull=&",
  "metric_1=&hfInn=&min_pitches=0&min_results=0&group_by=name&",
  "sort_col=pitches&player_event_sort=h_launch_speed&sort_order=desc&",
  "min_abs=0&type=details"
)

# Download to a temporary file
tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv")
GET(url, write_disk(tmp, overwrite = TRUE))

# Read CSV directly
bo_raw <- read_csv(tmp, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Keep only balls in play with valid spray coordinates
bo_bip <- bo_raw %>%
  filter(type == "X", !is.na(hc_x), !is.na(hc_y)) %>%
  select(
    player_name,
    game_date,
    events,
    stand,
    batter,
    pitcher,
    hc_x,
    hc_y,
    launch_speed,
    launch_angle,
    hit_distance_sc
  )

# Save cleaned file
write_csv(bo_bip, "bo_bichette_2023_bip.csv")

# Preview
print(head(bo_bip))
print(nrow(bo_bip))
```

```{r Spray_chart_no_diamond, include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
bo_bip <- read_csv("bo_bichette_2023_bip.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

ggplot(bo_bip, aes(x = hc_x, y = -hc_y)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  coord_fixed() +
  xlim(0, 500) +
  ylim(-275, 0) +
  labs(
    title = "Bo Bichette 2023 Spray Chart",
    x = "Statcast x-coordinate",
    y = "Statcast y-coordinate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
bo_plot <- bo_bip %>%
  filter(!is.na(hc_x), !is.na(hc_y)) %>%
  mutate(
    spray_x = hc_x - 125,
    spray_y = 200 - hc_y,
    result_class = case_when(
      events %in% c("single") ~ "Single",
      events %in% c("double") ~ "Double",
      events %in% c("triple") ~ "Triple",
      events %in% c("home_run") ~ "Home Run",
      events %in% c("field_out", "force_out", "grounded_into_double_play",
                    "double_play", "triple_play", "fielders_choice_out",
                    "sac_fly", "sac_bunt", "other_out") ~ "Out",
      events %in% c("field_error") ~ "Error",
      is.na(events) ~ "Other",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )

# Field geometry
foul_lines <- data.frame(
  x = c(0, -150, 0, 150),
  y = c(0, 150, 0, 150),
  grp = c("L", "L", "R", "R")
)

theta_in <- seq(pi/4, 3*pi/4, length.out = 200)
infield_arc <- data.frame(
  x = 95 * cos(theta_in),
  y = 95 * sin(theta_in)
)

theta_out <- seq(pi/4, 3*pi/4, length.out = 300)
outfield_arc <- data.frame(
  x = 220 * cos(theta_out),
  y = 220 * sin(theta_out)
)

# helper function to make a rotated square (diamond-shaped base)
make_base <- function(cx, cy, s = 6, name = "base") {
  data.frame(
    x = c(cx, cx + s, cx, cx - s, cx),
    y = c(cy + s, cy, cy - s, cy, cy + s),
    base = name
  )
}

first_base  <- make_base(63.64, 63.64, s = 6, name = "First")
second_base <- make_base(0, 127.28, s = 6, name = "Second")
third_base  <- make_base(-63.64, 63.64, s = 6, name = "Third")

bases_poly <- rbind(first_base, second_base, third_base)

mound <- data.frame(
  x = 0,
  y = 60.5
)

home_plate <- data.frame(
  x = c(-8, -8, 0, 8, 8, -8),
  y = c(0, 8, 14, 8, 0, 0)
)

ggplot() +
  geom_path(data = outfield_arc, aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_path(data = infield_arc, aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_line(data = subset(foul_lines, grp == "L"), aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_line(data = subset(foul_lines, grp == "R"), aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_polygon(
    data = bases_poly,
    aes(x = x, y = y, group = base),
    fill = "white",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.9,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_polygon(
    data = home_plate,
    aes(x = x, y = y),
    fill = "white",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.9,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(data = mound, aes(x = x, y = y), size = 2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_point(
    data = bo_plot,
    aes(x = spray_x, y = spray_y, color = result_class),
    alpha = 0.7,
    size = 2.2
  ) +
  coord_fixed() +
  xlim(-160, 160) +
  ylim(0, 230) +
  labs(
    title = "Bo Bichette 2023 Spray Chart",
    subtitle = "Balls in play classified by result",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    color = "Result"
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10)
  )
```

```{r Bo_Bichette_KDE, include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot() +
  # density layer first
  stat_density_2d(
    data = bo_plot,
    aes(x = spray_x, y = spray_y, fill = after_stat(level), alpha = after_stat(level)),
    geom = "polygon",
    contour = TRUE
  ) +
  
  # field geometry
  geom_path(data = outfield_arc, aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9, color = "black") +
  geom_path(data = infield_arc, aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9, color = "black") +
  geom_line(data = subset(foul_lines, grp == "L"), aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9, color = "black") +
  geom_line(data = subset(foul_lines, grp == "R"), aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9, color = "black") +
  geom_polygon(
    data = bases_poly,
    aes(x = x, y = y, group = base),
    fill = "white",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.9,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_polygon(
    data = home_plate,
    aes(x = x, y = y),
    fill = "white",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.9,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(data = mound, aes(x = x, y = y), size = 2, color = "black", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  
  # optional: add actual BIP points faintly on top
  geom_point(
    data = bo_plot,
    aes(x = spray_x, y = spray_y),
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.25,
    size = 1.2
  ) +
  
  coord_fixed() +
  xlim(-160, 160) +
  ylim(0, 230) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C", name = "Density") +
  scale_alpha(range = c(0.15, 0.7), guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Bo Bichette 2023 Kernel Density Map",
    subtitle = "Density of balls in play",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    legend.position = "right"
  )
```
```{r Bo_Bichette_9_Zone_ESDA, include=FALSE}
library(dplyr)

# Create 3 x 3 grid using bo_plot coordinates
bo_zones <- bo_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(spray_x), !is.na(spray_y)) %>%
  mutate(
    x_zone = case_when(
      spray_x <= quantile(spray_x, 1/3, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Left",
      spray_x <= quantile(spray_x, 2/3, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Center",
      TRUE ~ "Right"
    ),
    y_zone = case_when(
      spray_y <= quantile(spray_y, 1/3, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Shallow",
      spray_y <= quantile(spray_y, 2/3, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Medium",
      TRUE ~ "Deep"
    ),
    zone_9 = paste(y_zone, x_zone, sep = "_")
  )

# Count observations in each zone
zone_counts_9 <- bo_zones %>%
  count(y_zone, x_zone, zone_9) %>%
  mutate(proportion = n / sum(n)) %>%
  arrange(
    factor(y_zone, levels = c("Deep", "Medium", "Shallow")),
    factor(x_zone, levels = c("Left", "Center", "Right"))
  )

print(zone_counts_9)

# Chi-square goodness-of-fit test
# Null hypothesis: balls in play are evenly distributed across the 9 zones
chisq_result_9 <- chisq.test(zone_counts_9$n)

print(chisq_result_9)

# Show observed vs expected counts
expected_counts_9 <- data.frame(
  zone_9 = zone_counts_9$zone_9,
  observed = zone_counts_9$n,
  expected = round(chisq_result_9$expected, 2)
)

print(expected_counts_9)
```

The p-value is very low $4*10^{-9}$ we reject the null hypothesis that balls are evenly distriubted across the 9 zones identifying a non-uniform distribution. 



```{r Bo_Bichette_Morans_I, include=FALSE}
# Spatial autocorrelation analysis for Bo Bichette BIP locations
# Uses a fine field grid rather than only the 9-zone grid

library(sf)
library(spdep)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

bo_points_sf <- bo_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(spray_x), !is.na(spray_y)) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("spray_x", "spray_y"))

# Create approximate fair-territory field polygon
theta_field <- seq(pi / 4, 3 * pi / 4, length.out = 200)

field_boundary <- data.frame(
  x = c(0, 220 * cos(theta_field), 0),
  y = c(0, 220 * sin(theta_field), 0)
)

field_poly <- st_sfc(
  st_polygon(list(as.matrix(field_boundary)))
)

# Create grid over fair territory
n_grid_x <- 10
n_grid_y <- 10

field_grid <- st_make_grid(
  field_poly,
  n = c(n_grid_x, n_grid_y),
  what = "polygons"
) %>%
  st_sf(cell_id = 1:length(.), geometry = .)

# Keep grid cells whose centroids fall inside fair territory
field_grid <- field_grid[st_intersects(st_centroid(field_grid), field_poly, sparse = FALSE), ]

# Count BIP points in each grid cell
point_counts <- st_join(bo_points_sf, field_grid, join = st_within) %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  count(cell_id, name = "bip_count")

field_grid_counts <- field_grid %>%
  left_join(point_counts, by = "cell_id") %>%
  mutate(
    bip_count = ifelse(is.na(bip_count), 0, bip_count)
  )

# Create spatial neighbors and weights
neighbors <- poly2nb(field_grid_counts, queen = TRUE)
weights <- nb2listw(neighbors, style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE)

# -----------------------------
# Global Moran's I
# -----------------------------

global_moran <- moran.test(
  field_grid_counts$bip_count,
  weights,
  zero.policy = TRUE
)

print(global_moran)

# -----------------------------
# Local Moran's I
# -----------------------------

local_moran <- localmoran(
  field_grid_counts$bip_count,
  weights,
  zero.policy = TRUE
)

field_grid_counts <- field_grid_counts %>%
  mutate(
    local_I = local_moran[, "Ii"],
    local_p = local_moran[, "Pr(z != E(Ii))"],
    count_z = as.numeric(scale(bip_count)),
    lag_count = lag.listw(weights, bip_count, zero.policy = TRUE),
    lag_z = as.numeric(scale(lag_count)),
    lisa_cluster = case_when(
      local_p < 0.05 & count_z > 0 & lag_z > 0 ~ "High-High",
      local_p < 0.05 & count_z < 0 & lag_z < 0 ~ "Low-Low",
      local_p < 0.05 & count_z > 0 & lag_z < 0 ~ "High-Low",
      local_p < 0.05 & count_z < 0 & lag_z > 0 ~ "Low-High",
      TRUE ~ "Not Significant"
    )
  )

# View local Moran results
field_grid_counts %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(cell_id, bip_count, local_I, local_p, lisa_cluster) %>%
  arrange(local_p) %>%
  print()

# -----------------------------
# Map Local Moran clusters
# -----------------------------

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(
    data = field_grid_counts,
    aes(fill = lisa_cluster),
    color = "white",
    linewidth = 0.25
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = bo_plot,
    aes(x = spray_x, y = spray_y),
    alpha = 0.35,
    size = 1
  ) +
  geom_path(data = outfield_arc, aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_path(data = infield_arc, aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_line(data = subset(foul_lines, grp == "L"), aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_line(data = subset(foul_lines, grp == "R"), aes(x = x, y = y), linewidth = 0.9) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-160, 160), ylim = c(0, 230), expand = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Bo Bichette 2023 Local Moran's I Cluster Map",
    subtitle = "Spatial autocorrelation of balls in play by field grid cell",
    fill = "LISA Cluster"
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 15),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
    legend.position = "right"
  )
```


```{r Bo_Bichette_Ripleys_K, include=FALSE}
# Ripley's K / L Function Point Pattern Analysis for Bo Bichette
# This tests whether Bo Bichette's BIP locations are clustered across multiple spatial scales.

library(spatstat.geom)
library(spatstat.explore)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Prepare Bo Bichette BIP coordinates
bo_xy <- bo_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(spray_x), !is.na(spray_y)) %>%
  select(spray_x, spray_y)

# Create approximate fair-territory window
theta_field <- seq(pi / 4, 3 * pi / 4, length.out = 300)

field_window_coords <- data.frame(
  x = c(0, 220 * cos(theta_field), 0),
  y = c(0, 220 * sin(theta_field), 0)
)

field_window <- owin(
  poly = list(
    x = field_window_coords$x,
    y = field_window_coords$y
  )
)

# Keep only points inside the field window
inside_field <- inside.owin(
  x = bo_xy$spray_x,
  y = bo_xy$spray_y,
  w = field_window
)

bo_xy_in <- bo_xy[inside_field, ]

# Create point pattern object
bo_ppp <- ppp(
  x = bo_xy_in$spray_x,
  y = bo_xy_in$spray_y,
  window = field_window
)

# Optional: check the point pattern
summary(bo_ppp)

# -----------------------------
# Ripley's K Function
# -----------------------------

bo_k <- Kest(
  bo_ppp,
  correction = "border"
)

plot(
  bo_k,
  main = "Bo Bichette 2023 Ripley's K Function",
  legend = TRUE
)

# -----------------------------
# Ripley's L Function
# Easier to interpret than K.
# If observed L is above theoretical L, points are clustered.
# -----------------------------

bo_l <- Lest(
  bo_ppp,
  correction = "border"
)

plot(
  bo_l,
  main = "Bo Bichette 2023 Ripley's L Function",
  legend = TRUE
)

# -----------------------------
# Monte Carlo Envelope
# Tests whether observed clustering differs from complete spatial randomness
# -----------------------------

set.seed(123)

bo_l_envelope <- envelope(
  bo_ppp,
  fun = Lest,
  nsim = 99,
  correction = "border",
  global = FALSE
)

plot(
  bo_l_envelope,
  main = "Bo Bichette 2023 Ripley's L Function with CSR Envelope",
  legend = TRUE
)

# -----------------------------
# Convert L results to a clean dataframe for ggplot
# -----------------------------

bo_l_df <- data.frame(
  distance = bo_l$r,
  observed_L = bo_l$border,
  theoretical_L = bo_l$theo
)

ggplot(bo_l_df, aes(x = distance)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = observed_L), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = theoretical_L), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Bo Bichette 2023 Ripley's L Function",
    subtitle = "Observed spatial clustering compared to complete spatial randomness",
    x = "Distance",
    y = "L(r)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# -----------------------------
# Convert envelope results to dataframe for cleaner plot
# -----------------------------

bo_l_envelope_df <- data.frame(
  distance = bo_l_envelope$r,
  observed_L = bo_l_envelope$obs,
  theoretical_L = bo_l_envelope$theo,
  lower = bo_l_envelope$lo,
  upper = bo_l_envelope$hi
)

ggplot(bo_l_envelope_df, aes(x = distance)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(aes(y = observed_L), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = theoretical_L), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Bo Bichette 2023 Ripley's L Function with Monte Carlo Envelope",
    subtitle = "Observed clustering across distances compared to simulated randomness",
    x = "Distance",
    y = "L(r)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r directional_statistics}
# -----------------------------
# Directional Statistics / Spray Angle Modeling
# -----------------------------

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Create spray angle variable
# Negative angles = pull side
# Positive angles = opposite field

bo_angles <- bo_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(spray_x), !is.na(spray_y)) %>%
  mutate(
    spray_angle_rad = atan2(spray_x, spray_y),
    spray_angle_deg = spray_angle_rad * 180 / pi
  )

# Mean spray angle
bo_angles %>%
  summarize(
    mean_spray_angle = mean(spray_angle_deg, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```

```{r histogram_spray_angles}
# -----------------------------
# Histogram of Spray Angles
# -----------------------------

ggplot(bo_angles, aes(x = spray_angle_deg)) +
  geom_histogram(
    bins = 30,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Bo Bichette 2023 Spray Angle Distribution",
    subtitle = "Negative angles indicate pull-side contact; positive angles indicate opposite-field contact",
    x = "Spray Angle (degrees)",
    y = "Number of Balls in Play"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r desnsity_plot_spray_angles}
# -----------------------------
# Density Plot of Spray Angles
# -----------------------------

ggplot(bo_angles, aes(x = spray_angle_deg)) +
  geom_density(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Bo Bichette 2023 Spray Angle Density",
    subtitle = "Smoothed distribution of batted-ball spray angles",
    x = "Spray Angle (degrees)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r spray_direction_classification}
# -----------------------------
# Spray Direction Classification
# -----------------------------

bo_angles <- bo_angles %>%
  mutate(
    spray_direction_model = case_when(
      spray_angle_deg < -15 ~ "Pull",
      spray_angle_deg >= -15 & spray_angle_deg <= 15 ~ "Center",
      spray_angle_deg > 15 ~ "Opposite"
    )
  )

bo_direction_model_summary <- bo_angles %>%
  count(spray_direction_model) %>%
  mutate(
    percent = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)
  )

bo_direction_model_summary
```

```{r bar_chart_of_spray_direction}
# -----------------------------
# Bar Chart of Spray Direction
# -----------------------------

ggplot(
  bo_direction_model_summary,
  aes(x = spray_direction_model, y = percent)
) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Bo Bichette 2023 Modeled Spray Direction",
    subtitle = "Classification based on spray angle cutoffs",
    x = "Spray Direction",
    y = "Percent of Balls in Play"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r one_sample_mean_test}
# -----------------------------
# One-Sample Test on Mean Spray Angle
# -----------------------------
# Null hypothesis:
# Mean spray angle = 0 degrees
# (perfectly centered spray distribution)

bo_angle_ttest <- t.test(
  bo_angles$spray_angle_deg,
  mu = 0
)

bo_angle_ttest
```
